name: Léopoldville
notes: Urban district around the capital. By 1954 Léopoldville had grown into the largest city in sub-Saharan Africa.

name: Lac Léopold II
notes: Named after Lake Léopold II (now Lake Mai-Ndombe). Later became Mai-Ndombe District.

name: Kwilu
notes: Named after the Kwilu River. Site of the Kwilu Rebellion (1963–1965) led by Pierre Mulele after independence.

name: Kwango
notes: Named after the Kwango River. Bordered Portuguese Angola.

name: Bas-Congo
notes: Lower Congo district including the Atlantic coast. Key port infrastructure at Matadi and Boma.

name: Cataractes
notes: Named for the cataracts (rapids) on the lower Congo River between Matadi and Kinshasa. An ancient crossroads region.

name: Lulua
notes: Named after the Lulua River. Later became part of Kasaï-Occidental province.

name: Sankuru
notes: Named after the Sankuru River. Later became Sankuru province in 2015.

name: Kabinda
notes: Named after the town of Kabinda. Later became Lomami province in 2015.

name: Kasai
notes: Core Kasai district. Major diamond deposits around Tshikapa.

name: Équateur
notes: Equatorial district in the central Congo basin. Dense tropical rainforest.

name: Mongala
notes: Named after the Mongala River. Later became Mongala province in 2015. Birthplace region of Mobutu Sese Seko.

name: Ubangi
notes: Named after the Ubangi River. Later split into Nord-Ubangi and Sud-Ubangi provinces in 2015.

name: Tshuapa
notes: Named after the Tshuapa River. Remote rainforest region. Later became Tshuapa province in 2015.

name: Stanleyville
notes: Centered on Stanleyville (now Kisangani). Major river port and trading center. Later became Tshopo province in 2015.

name: Ituri
notes: Named after the Ituri River and Forest. Home to the Mbuti pygmy peoples. Later became Ituri province in 2015.

name: Bas-Uele
notes: Lower Uele district. Later became Bas-Uélé province in 2015.

name: Haut-Uele
notes: Upper Uele district. Later became Haut-Uélé province in 2015. Region of Lord's Resistance Army activity in the 2000s.

name: Sud-Kivu
notes: Southern Kivu district bordering Lake Tanganyika and Rwanda. Later became Sud-Kivu province.

name: Nord-Kivu
notes: Northern Kivu district bordering Lake Edward and Uganda. Home to Virunga volcanoes. Later became Nord-Kivu province.

name: Maniema
notes: Interior district west of Kivu. Historically the zone of Arab-Swahili influence (Tippu Tip). Later became Maniema province.

name: Élisabethville
notes: District around the copper capital Élisabethville (now Lubumbashi). Heart of the Union Minière du Haut-Katanga operations. Later Haut-Katanga province.

name: Tanganyika
notes: Named after Lake Tanganyika. Eastern Katanga along the lake shore. Later became Tanganyika province in 2015.

name: Lualaba
notes: Named after the Lualaba River (upper Congo). Mining district. Later became Lualaba province in 2015.

name: Haut-Lomami
notes: Upper Lomami district. Later became Haut-Lomami province in 2015.

name: Luapula-Moero
notes: Named after the Luapula River and Lake Moero (Mweru). Southeastern Katanga bordering Northern Rhodesia (now Zambia).
